description: Six-phase fund lifecycle pipeline covering formation, capital raise, deployment, monitoring, distributions, and exit/wind-down. Coordinates 22 specialist agents across sequential and cyclical phases to produce a terminal verdict of DEPLOY, HOLD, or WIND-DOWN. Manages GP economics (management fees, carried interest, co-invest, clawback, organizational expenses) across the full fund lifecycle. Phase 4 (monitoring) is recurring quarterly; Phase 5 (distributions) is event-driven by asset sales, refinancing proceeds, or scheduled distribution events. Terminal deliverable is either a confirmed deployment continuation, a quarterly LP performance report, or a handoff to disposition for wind-down sequencing.

outputs: fund structure recommendation (LP/GP, parallel vehicles, feeder funds)
GP economics framework (management fee, carry, co-invest, clawback)
fund governance structure (LPAC, key man provisions, excuse/exclusion rights)
regulatory pathway analysis (Advisers Act, ERISA, CFIUS)

validationRules.description: GP economics framework must define management fee rate and basis, carry rate, hurdle rate, catch-up provision, and co-invest commitment.
Fund structure must specify entity type, domicile, tax treatment, and parallel vehicle requirements.
Governance structure must include LPAC composition, key man provisions, no-fault removal threshold, and excuse/exclusion mechanics.

outputs: side letter matrix (MFN provisions, fee breaks, co-invest rights)
regulatory filing checklist (Form D, Form PF, blue sky)

validationRules.description: LPA key terms must cover fund term, investment period, extensions, distribution waterfall, clawback, key man, removal, and excuse/exclusion.
Side letter matrix must identify at least 5 standard MFN provisions and track which LPs receive which concessions.
Regulatory filing checklist must identify all required federal and state filings with deadlines.

outputs: concentration limits (geography, asset type, single-asset, vintage)
leverage policy (fund-level and asset-level LTV caps)
return targets (gross IRR, net IRR, equity multiple, cash yield)
risk parameters (max loss tolerance, liquidity reserve, hedge ratio)

validationRules.description: All concentration limits must have numerical thresholds (not qualitative guidance) for geography, asset type, single-asset, and vintage.
Return targets must be expressed as gross and net IRR with explicit spread over relevant benchmark (NCREIF, Cambridge).
Leverage policy must specify fund-level aggregate LTV cap and per-asset LTV cap with recourse limitations.

outputs: tax opinion (pass-through treatment, UBTI, ECI, FIRPTA)
ERISA plan asset analysis (25% test, VCOC, REOC exemptions)
securities law compliance checklist (Reg D, Reg S, qualified purchaser)

validationRules.description: ERISA plan asset analysis must address the 25% test, VCOC exemption eligibility, and REOC exemption eligibility.
Tax opinion must confirm pass-through treatment and identify UBTI, ECI, and FIRPTA exposure for each investor category.
Securities law checklist must confirm exemption basis (506(b) or 506(c)) and identify qualified purchaser vs accredited investor thresholds.

outputs: tax-efficient structure recommendation (blocker entities, parallel funds, feeder structures)
withholding obligation matrix (FIRPTA, ECI, portfolio interest)
depreciation strategy (cost segregation, bonus depreciation, MACRS)
carried interest tax treatment analysis (3-year holding period requirement)

validationRules.description: Tax structure must address whether blocker entities are needed for tax-exempt and foreign investors.
Withholding obligation matrix must cover FIRPTA, ECI, and portfolio interest for each investor category.
Analysis must confirm whether carried interest qualifies for long-term capital gains treatment under the 3-year holding period requirement.

passConditions.description: Fund structure designer, legal docs coordinator, investment policy drafter, and fund counsel all completed successfully.
Management fee schedule, carry structure, co-invest commitment, clawback mechanics, and organizational expense cap are all quantified.
LPA key terms sheet covers all required provisions: fund term, investment period, waterfall, clawback, governance, and transfers.
Investment policy statement has quantified concentration limits, leverage caps, and return targets benchmarked to industry standards.

conditionalConditions.description: Tax structure advisor produced partial analysis; blocker entity or withholding analysis pending outside counsel review.
Side letter matrix is incomplete; certain MFN provisions require LP-by-LP negotiation during capital raise.

failConditions.description: Fund cannot qualify for VCOC or REOC ERISA exemption, blocking plan asset investors without restructuring.
Fund structure does not qualify for any Reg D exemption; public registration required.
One or more critical formation agents failed after all retry attempts.

fundStructure.description: Complete fund structure including entity type, domicile, parallel vehicles, feeder structures, and tax treatment. Seeds capital raise materials and all downstream phases.

gpEconomics.description: GP economics framework: management fee (rate, basis, step-down), carry (rate, hurdle, catch-up, whole-fund vs deal-by-deal), co-invest commitment, clawback mechanics, and organizational expense cap. Tracked across all subsequent phases.

lpaTerms.description: LPA key terms including fund term, investment period, extensions, waterfall mechanics, governance, and transfer restrictions.

investmentPolicy.description: Investment policy statement with concentration limits, leverage caps, return targets, and risk parameters. Governs all deployment decisions.

regulatoryCompliance.description: Regulatory filing checklist and compliance status. ERISA analysis, securities law exemption basis, and AML/KYC framework.

taxStructure.description: Tax-efficient structure recommendations including blocker analysis, withholding matrix, and depreciation strategy.

crossAgentValidations.description: GP economics defined by fund structure designer must match the LPA key terms produced by legal docs coordinator.
Leverage limits in the investment policy must be consistent with the fund structure's stated leverage philosophy.
Legal structure opinion and tax structure recommendation must not conflict on entity type, domicile, or blocker requirements.

outputs: LP pitch deck (strategy, team, track record, terms, pipeline)
data room structure and population checklist
competitive positioning analysis vs comparable funds

validationRules.description: Pitch deck must include all required sections: executive summary, strategy, team, track record, pipeline, terms, risk factors, and appendix.
GP track record data must include at least gross and net IRR, equity multiple, and number of investments for all prior funds.
Fund terms presented in pitch deck must exactly match LPA key terms from formation phase.

inputs: target LP list with allocation preferences
outputs: capital raise pipeline tracker (soft circle, hard commit, subscription)
LP meeting schedule and follow-up log

validationRules.description: Capital raise pipeline must track each LP prospect with status (contacted, meeting held, soft circle, hard commit, subscribed) and commitment amount.
First close threshold must be quantified as both dollar amount and percentage of target fund size.
Fundraise timeline must have target dates for first close, subsequent closes, and final close with actual vs target tracking.
DDQ response package must cover all standard ILPA DDQ sections: strategy, governance, operations, ESG, track record, and conflicts.
Every side letter request must be logged with LP name, provision requested, GP response, and MFN impact analysis.
Every subscribing LP must have AML/KYC verification completed before subscription is accepted.
Every subscribing LP must be verified as accredited investor or qualified purchaser per the fund's exemption basis.
Sum of all LP commitments in the capital commitment register must equal total fund commitments reported in the pipeline tracker.

requiredDataKeys.description: Fund structure required for pitch deck and LP materials.
GP economics framework required for pitch deck terms section and side letter negotiations.
LPA key terms required for subscription agreement and side letter matrix.
Investment policy required for pitch deck strategy section and LP DDQ responses.
Regulatory compliance status required for subscription processing and investor qualification.

passConditions.description: Total hard commitments plus subscriptions meet or exceed the first close threshold.
All subscribing LPs have completed AML/KYC and investor qualification verification.
Official capital commitment register is balanced and reconciled to subscription documents.

conditionalConditions.description: Total commitments are above first close minimum but below target fund size; subsequent closes planned.
Some side letter negotiations are still in progress but do not block first close.

failConditions.description: Total commitments do not meet the minimum first close threshold.
Material AML/KYC issues remain unresolved for one or more subscribing LPs.

capitalCommitmentRegister.description: Official capital commitment register with LP names, commitment amounts, close dates, and side letter provisions. Seeds capital call processing and LP capital account initialization.

deployableCapital.description: Total deployable capital after deducting management fees, organizational expenses, and fund expense reserves from total commitments.

lpRoster.description: LP roster with contact information, side letter provisions, LPAC membership, and reporting preferences.

managementFeeSchedule.description: Management fee accrual schedule starting from first close date with quarterly calculation and annual reconciliation.

fundraiseStatus.description: FIRST_CLOSE, SUBSEQUENT_CLOSE, FINAL_CLOSE, or HARD_CAP. Indicates whether additional capital raises are planned.

crossAgentValidations.description: Capital commitment register from subscription processor must match the hard commit totals in the capital raise pipeline tracker.
Fund terms in pitch deck must match terms in subscription agreements.

outputs: deployment pacing model (target vs actual by quarter)
portfolio construction plan (target allocation by geography, asset type, risk profile)
management fee drag analysis (uninvested capital cost)
recycling strategy (reinvestment of early proceeds during investment period)

validationRules.description: Deployment pacing model must have quarterly targets for the full investment period with actual-vs-target tracking.
Portfolio construction plan must demonstrate compliance with all investment policy concentration limits.
J-curve projection must show expected negative return trough depth and estimated quarters to breakeven.
Management fee drag on uninvested capital must be quantified in basis points of gross return impact.
Every deal allocation must pass all investment policy concentration checks. No single deal may breach any limit.
Capital call amount must not exceed uncalled commitments net of excuse/exclusion provisions.
Portfolio-level risk/return attribution must be recalculated after each new allocation.

inputs: LP co-invest rights from side letters
deal size relative to fund allocation limit
outputs: co-invest allocation matrix (LP eligibility, allocated amount, terms)
co-invest fee and carry terms (if different from fund)

validationRules.description: All LPs with contractual co-invest rights per side letters must receive co-invest notice before general co-invest offering.
GP co-invest must be sized and timed pari passu with LP capital per LPA terms.
Co-invest fee and carry terms must be documented and consistent with LPA provisions on co-invest economics.

requiredDataKeys.description: Capital commitment register required for deployment pacing and capital call sizing.
Deployable capital required for portfolio construction and allocation decisions.
LP roster required for co-invest coordination and excuse/exclusion tracking.
Management fee schedule needed for fee drag analysis on uninvested capital.

passConditions.description: Actual deployment is within 20% of pacing target for the current quarter.
Portfolio is compliant with all investment policy concentration limits after latest allocations.
All capital calls for deployed deals have been issued and funded.

conditionalConditions.description: Actual deployment is 20-40% below pacing target; pipeline is active but closing timelines are extended.
Co-invest allocations are in process but LP responses are not yet received for one or more deals.

failConditions.description: Actual deployment is more than 40% below pacing target with no near-term pipeline.
Portfolio has breached one or more investment policy concentration limits.

portfolioComposition.description: Current portfolio composition by geography, asset type, risk profile, and vintage. Seeds monitoring phase portfolio-level reporting.

deploymentStatus.description: Deployed vs remaining capacity, pacing status, and capital call history.

dealAllocations.description: Per-deal allocation amounts, dates, and return targets. Seeds performance attribution in monitoring phase.

coInvestAllocations.description: Co-invest allocations by LP and deal. Seeds co-invest reporting and performance tracking.

capitalCallHistory.description: Capital call notices issued with amounts, dates, and LP-level contribution status.

crossAgentValidations.description: Sum of deal allocations must equal the deployment strategist's reported deployed capital.
Deals flagged for co-invest by allocation analyst must appear in co-invest coordinator output.

inputs: per-asset performance data (NOI, occupancy, debt status)
market data by geography and asset type
outputs: fund-level performance dashboard (NAV, TVPI, DPI, RVPI, IRR)
per-asset performance attribution (contribution to fund return)
benchmark comparison (vs NCREIF, Cambridge, peer funds)
watch list (underperforming assets with intervention triggers)

validationRules.description: Fund NAV must be calculated as sum of all asset fair values minus fund-level liabilities.
TVPI, DPI, RVPI, and net IRR must all be calculated and non-null.
Sum of per-asset return contributions must reconcile to fund-level return within 10bps.
Benchmark comparison must use data no more than one quarter stale.

outputs: quarterly management fee calculation (rate x basis)
management fee basis determination (committed vs invested capital)
carried interest accrual (mark-to-market based on NAV)
carried interest waterfall position (cumulative tier analysis)

validationRules.description: Management fee basis must reflect committed capital during investment period and invested capital (or per LPA terms) after investment period.
Carried interest accrual must be calculated on cumulative fund returns above preferred return hurdle using the LPA-specified waterfall methodology.
Organizational expenses must be tracked against the LPA cap with remaining budget clearly stated.
Transaction fee offsets must be calculated at the LPA-specified offset rate and applied against management fee liability.
Clawback exposure must be calculated by comparing cumulative GP carry distributions to entitled carry based on current whole-fund returns.

outputs: investment policy compliance report (all concentration limits, leverage, strategy)
regulatory filing status (Form D amendments, Form PF, blue sky renewals)
side letter compliance certification per LP
conflict of interest log and resolution

validationRules.description: Every investment policy constraint must be tested with current portfolio data and compliance status reported.
ERISA 25% test must be calculated with current LP roster and benefit plan investor data.
All regulatory filings must be current or have future deadlines identified.
Side letter compliance must be certified for every LP with side letter provisions.

inputs: LP reporting preferences from side letters
outputs: quarterly investor letter (fund commentary, market update, portfolio highlights)

validationRules.description: NAV in the investor letter must match NAV from the performance analyst and the sum of all LP capital accounts.
Sum of all LP capital accounts plus GP capital must equal fund NAV.
TVPI, DPI, and IRR in investor letter must match performance analyst output.
LPs with custom reporting requirements per side letters must receive tailored reports.

requiredDataKeys.description: Current portfolio composition required for performance attribution and compliance testing.
Deployment status required for pacing reports and management fee basis determination.
Deal allocations required for per-asset performance attribution.
Capital call history required for LP capital account reconciliation and IRR calculation.

passConditions.description: Fund net IRR and TVPI are within 200bps and 0.2x of targets respectively.
Sum of LP capital accounts plus GP capital equals fund NAV within 0.01% tolerance.
No investment policy breaches and all regulatory filings current.
Quarterly investor letter and capital account statements produced for all LPs.

conditionalConditions.description: Fund performance is below target but above the intervention threshold. Watch list has assets flagged.
Fund is underperforming relevant benchmark by more than 200bps but portfolio thesis remains intact.

failConditions.description: LP capital accounts do not sum to fund NAV. Critical accounting error.
Investment policy breach identified with no cure plan approved.

fundPerformance.description: Fund-level performance metrics: NAV, TVPI, DPI, RVPI, net IRR, gross IRR. Seeds distribution waterfall and exit analysis.

gpEconomicsUpdate.description: Updated GP economics: management fees earned YTD, carry accrued, carry distributed, clawback exposure, fee offsets applied.

assetPerformance.description: Per-asset performance data: current NOI, occupancy, debt status, and return contribution. Seeds exit sequencing and distribution triggers.

watchList.description: Assets flagged for underperformance with intervention triggers and recommended actions.

complianceStatus.description: Investment policy compliance status, regulatory filing status, and side letter compliance certification.

quarterlyVerdict.description: ON_TRACK, WATCH, or IMPAIRED. Drives GP decision-making and LP communication tone.

crossAgentValidations.description: NAV from performance analyst, NAV used for carry accrual by fee calculator, and NAV reported in LP letter must all match.
TVPI, DPI, and net IRR in the investor letter must match performance analyst calculations.
Management fee basis used by fee calculator must align with compliance officer's determination of investment period status.

inputs: LP capital accounts (contributions, prior distributions, unreturned capital)
preferred return accrual data (per LP, compound/simple, annual/quarterly per LPA)
outputs: four-tier waterfall calculation (return of capital, preferred return, catch-up, residual)
cumulative distribution summary (total distributions, DPI, preferred return status)

validationRules.description: Total distributions across all tiers and all LPs plus GP must equal total available proceeds within $1 tolerance.
No waterfall tier may receive distributions before all prior tiers are fully satisfied.
Preferred return accrual must use the LPA-specified compounding method (simple vs compound, annual vs quarterly) and rate.
Clawback exposure must be calculated after every distribution event comparing cumulative GP carry to entitled carry.
Waterfall calculation must apply the correct methodology (whole-fund European or deal-by-deal American) per LPA terms.

inputs: capital call requirements (if simultaneous call for new deployment)
outputs: distribution notice per LP (amount, source, waterfall tier breakdown)
capital call notice (if applicable, with net settlement calculation)

validationRules.description: Distribution amounts in LP notices must exactly match the waterfall calculator output per LP.
Wire instructions must be verified for each LP before distribution notice is issued.
Post-distribution capital accounts must be calculated showing new unreturned capital balance per LP.

inputs: per-asset tax basis and depreciation schedule
outputs: income/gain character allocation per LP (ordinary, capital gain, Section 1231, depreciation recapture)
K-1 data update for distribution event

validationRules.description: Sum of all LP income/gain allocations must equal total fund income/gain for the distribution event.
Section 704(b) capital accounts must balance: beginning balance plus contributions plus allocable items minus distributions equals ending balance.
FIRPTA withholding must be calculated for every foreign LP receiving a USRPI gain distribution.

requiredDataKeys.description: Fund performance data required for waterfall tier determination.
GP economics status required for carry calculation and clawback assessment.
Asset performance data provides context for distribution source characterization.

passConditions.description: Total distributions equal total available proceeds. No rounding errors or unallocated amounts.
Distribution notices issued to all LPs with correct amounts and wire instructions.
Tax character allocations completed for all LPs including withholding calculations for foreign investors.

conditionalConditions.description: FIRPTA withholding calculations pending treaty analysis for certain foreign LPs. Distribution to domestic LPs can proceed.

failConditions.description: Waterfall calculation does not balance. Distributions cannot proceed.
Waterfall tier ordering violated. Prior tiers not fully satisfied before downstream tier distributions.

distributionRecord.description: Complete distribution record: per-LP amounts by waterfall tier, GP carry distributed, cumulative DPI.

postDistributionCapitalAccounts.description: Updated LP capital accounts reflecting distribution. Seeds next monitoring cycle and future distributions.

clawbackStatus.description: Current clawback exposure: cumulative GP carry distributions vs entitled carry. Triggers clawback resolution in wind-down.

taxAllocations.description: Income/gain character allocations per LP for K-1 preparation.

crossAgentValidations.description: Per-LP distribution amounts in notices must exactly match waterfall calculator output.
Total income/gain allocated by tax specialist must reconcile to the economic gain embedded in the distribution.

inputs: tax optimization considerations (1031, installment sale, tax lot ordering)
GP economics implications of exit ordering
outputs: exit sequence plan (ordered asset dispositions with target dates)
projected distribution schedule (quarterly distributions through wind-down)
extension requirement assessment (if exits extend beyond initial term)
minimum proceeds analysis per asset (floor prices for GP carry protection)
final fund performance projection (target TVPI, DPI, net IRR at dissolution)

validationRules.description: Every remaining portfolio asset must have an exit target date and projected proceeds in the sequence plan.
All exits must be scheduled within the fund term (plus approved extensions). Any asset requiring extension must be flagged.
Projected distribution schedule must show quarterly LP distributions through final dissolution.
Final fund performance must be projected showing TVPI, DPI, and net IRR at dissolution under base, bull, and bear scenarios.

inputs: GP economics status (carry accrued, distributed, clawback)
outputs: fund dissolution checklist (legal, regulatory, operational, financial)
wind-down expense budget and reserve adequacy analysis
regulatory de-registration timeline (Form ADV, state filings)

validationRules.description: Dissolution checklist must cover all legal, regulatory, operational, and financial items required for fund termination.
GP clawback must be calculated, resolved, and documented before final distributions.
Wind-down expense reserves must be sufficient to cover remaining fund expenses through dissolution.
Regulatory de-registration timeline must account for all federal and state filings with deadlines.

outputs: final year financial statements (draft for auditor)
complete distribution waterfall reconciliation (inception to dissolution)
GP economics final settlement (management fees, carry, co-invest, clawback)
final K-1 data package (all LPs, final year)
track record data package (for GP fundraising)

validationRules.description: Inception-to-dissolution waterfall must balance: total contributions = total distributions + remaining NAV (should be zero at dissolution).
GP economics must be fully settled: all management fees reconciled, carry finalized, clawback resolved, co-invest returned.
Final K-1 data package must be prepared for every LP with allocations for the final year and cumulative reconciliation.
Track record data must show gross and net IRR, TVPI, DPI calculated using audited financials and GIPS-compliant methodology.

requiredDataKeys.description: Complete distribution history required for final waterfall reconciliation.
Current LP capital accounts required for final distribution calculation.
Clawback status required for GP economics final settlement.
Tax allocation history required for final K-1 preparation.

passConditions.description: All portfolio assets have been disposed and proceeds distributed.
Inception-to-dissolution waterfall is fully balanced with zero remaining NAV.
All GP economics components are finalized including clawback resolution.
Final financial statements prepared and audit deliverable package complete.

conditionalConditions.description: One or more assets remain undisposed; extension of fund term is required.
Final audit is in progress but not yet completed.

failConditions.description: GP clawback calculation is disputed and resolution is blocked.
Final waterfall does not balance; dissolution cannot proceed.

finalVerdict.description: WOUND_DOWN (complete dissolution) or RESIDUAL (assets remaining, extension active).

trackRecordData.description: Fund track record: gross IRR, net IRR, TVPI, DPI, fund size, vintage, strategy, number of investments. For GP fundraising.

gpEconomicsFinal.description: Final GP economics: total management fees, total carry, total co-invest return, clawback resolution. GP track record.

dissolutionStatus.description: Dissolution status: legal entity status, regulatory de-registration status, insurance tail, and indemnification.

crossAgentValidations.description: Exit timeline from exit sequencer must align with dissolution timeline from wind-down coordinator.
Clawback resolution from wind-down coordinator must match final carry settlement in audit package.

verdictValues.DEPLOY: Fund is deploying capital on pace, performance on track, and GP economics healthy. Continue deployment and monitoring cycles.
verdictValues.HOLD: Fund is pausing new deployment due to market conditions, concentration limits, or pacing concerns. Existing portfolio continues monitoring.
verdictValues.WIND-DOWN: Fund has entered wind-down period. Exit sequencing active, final distributions pending, dissolution in progress.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Investment strategy orchestrator produces fund strategy recommendation

targetAllocation.description: Target portfolio allocation by geography, asset type, risk profile, and vintage from the investment strategy orchestrator.

returnTargets.description: Return targets (gross IRR, net IRR, equity multiple, cash yield) from the investment strategy orchestrator.

riskBudget.description: Risk budget parameters (max loss, liquidity reserve, concentration limits) from the investment strategy orchestrator.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Disposition pipeline closes a sale on a fund asset

saleProceeds.description: Net sale proceeds from the disposition for fund NAV update and distribution waterfall.

distributionWaterfall.description: Deal-level distribution waterfall from the disposition for LP distribution calculation.

taxImpact.description: Tax impact data: capital gains, depreciation recapture, 1031 exchange status.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Hold period monitor produces quarterly performance data for a fund asset

propertyPerformance.description: Quarterly property performance data for fund-level portfolio monitoring.

currentNOI.description: Current quarter annualized NOI for fund NAV and performance attribution.

occupancy.description: Current physical occupancy rate for portfolio risk monitoring.

debtStatus.description: Loan covenant status, DSCR, and maturity schedule for fund-level debt monitoring.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Phase 'deployment' approves a deal for fund allocation

fundDeploymentCapacity.description: Remaining fund deployment capacity for the acquisition.

allocationTarget.description: Target equity allocation for the specific deal from the fund.

strategyConstraints.description: Fund-level strategy constraints that apply to the deal: leverage cap, return target, concentration limit compliance status.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Phase 'deployment' approves a development deal for fund allocation

constructionAllocationBudget.description: Approved equity allocation for construction/development from the fund.

fundLeveragePolicy.description: Fund leverage policy constraints for the development deal: max LTC, recourse limitations, completion guarantee requirements.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Phase 'monitoring' produces quarterly fund performance data

fundNAV.description: Current fund NAV for portfolio-level aggregation.

TVPI.description: Total value to paid-in multiple for portfolio performance tracking.

DPI.description: Distributions to paid-in multiple for portfolio cash return tracking.

assetLevelPerformance.description: Per-asset performance data for portfolio-level risk monitoring and rebalancing.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Phase 'exit-wind-down' initiates asset dispositions during fund wind-down

exitSequencePriority.description: Priority order in exit sequence (1 = exit first). Drives disposition pipeline scheduling.

targetExitDate.description: Target exit date from the exit sequence plan. Constrains disposition timeline.

minimumProceeds.description: Minimum acceptable sale proceeds based on fund return requirements and GP carry protection.
